changes.detail: All five mandatory fields present: work_id=Q-1, branch=feature/questionnaire-ux-redesign, risk_level=HIGH, execution_model=TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH, change_type=BEHAVIOUR. BEHAVIOUR is correct — the sprint changes how the form renders, navigates, validates, and presents input controls. HIGH is conservative for a frontend-only component but GPT has architecture authority; no counter-evidence justifies overriding it. HIGH classification requires dual approval before merge.

Sole file in scope. File exists. Props interface at lines 31-36 is identical to the interface specified in the prompt. The component loads schema from fetchQuestionnaireSchema() (questionnaireSchema.ts), which fetches from /api/questionnaire/schema and validates type compatibility via assertQuestionnaireSchemaCompatible(). Cursor must not change this loading mechanism.

Upload page exists and uses QuestionnaireForm via import at line 8. Correctly listed as out-of-scope. Must require zero modification after redesign. Integration is through the unchanged props interface only.

conditionalDisplay interface confirmed: { dependsOn: string; showWhen: string[] }. Current QuestionnaireForm.tsx does NOT read or act on conditionalDisplay — all questions render unconditionally. The prompt requirement to 'preserve conditional logic' is therefore an IMPLEMENTATION requirement, not a preservation requirement. Cursor must filter each question through: if (q.conditionalDisplay && !q.conditionalDisplay.showWhen.includes(String(responses[q.conditionalDisplay.dependsOn]))) hide question. Responses for hidden questions must not be cleared from state — only hidden from view — to preserve payload integrity.

Section display names, emojis, and time estimates in prompt match the background specification exactly. Seven sections in schema order: demographics, medical_history, symptoms, lifestyle, physical_assessment, cognitive_assessment, family_history. Total estimate ~13 min confirmed as sum of per-section estimates. Background doc is available at docs/QUESTIONNAIRE_UX_REDESIGN_BACKGROUND.md for Cursor reference.

Render/validate mismatch confirmed. Line 468: render uses getQuestionsBySection() — renders all sections at once. Line 86-90: getCurrentQuestions() slices 5 questions at a time used only for validation. The step counter (currentStep) controls validation slice but not visible content. Redesign must replace both functions with section-aware rendering and section-aware validation aligned to the active section.

?autofill=true dev shortcut exists at lines 77-84. Uses dynamic import of @/lib/mock/questionnaire. Must be preserved verbatim in redesign — only the state setter target changes (still setResponses). No logic change required.

onSubmit receives responses: Record<string,unknown> — a flat map of questionId to value. New section-based navigation must not partition responses into nested section objects. All question IDs from all sections must accumulate in a single flat responses map regardless of which section is currently active. Hidden conditional questions must retain their response value in state (not be cleared on hide). This is the critical payload parity risk — fully covered by AC-7 and STOP #1.

AC targets verified: (1) intent screen before questions; (2) 7 sections on intent screen with names+estimates; (3) one section at a time; (4) completed/active section states; (5) pill tiles for <=6 options; (6) section-advance validation; (7) payload unchanged; (8) onCancel from intent screen; (9) autofill; (10) conditionalDisplay logic; (11) no TS errors; (12) upload page unmodified. These 12 AC items are complete and testable.

advisory_notes: CONDITIONAL DISPLAY IS NEW IMPLEMENTATION: The prompt says 'preserve' but the current component has zero conditionalDisplay handling. Cursor must implement it from scratch using questionnaireSchema.ts:32-35 interface. Do not skip this — menstrual_hormonal_status and low_testosterone_symptoms questions are conditional on biological_sex and must not render for the wrong sex.

DESIGN CONTEXT: 'Claude already understands the design intent' (prompt line 17) refers to session context Cursor does not share. Full design specification is at docs/QUESTIONNAIRE_UX_REDESIGN_BACKGROUND.md. Cursor should read this file before implementing the intent screen and section layout.

PILL TILE THRESHOLD: The <= 6 options rule applies to dropdowns and checkboxes. For reference: biological_sex (3 options), ethnicity (7 options — use searchable select), dietary_pattern, tobacco_use, sleep_quality_rating (slider — keep slider). Cursor must count options from the live schema, not assume.

HIGH RISK MERGE GATE: This is classified HIGH. Dual approval required before merge to main. Claude audit complete. GPT architectural review implicit (GPT authored prompt). Second approval must be obtained before merge.
